verifyCommand: uv run --extra vision --extra graph pytest && uvx ruff check .
acceptanceCriteria: Verification passes (uv run --extra vision --extra graph pytest && uvx ruff check .)
All non-skipped features completed (passes: true)
All pre-existing tests still pass unchanged
notes: LOOP 4 — a multi-relational fraud-ring GRAPH layer (NetworkX, new `graph` extra). THESIS: fraud over these PPP loans is COORDINATED/RELATIONAL, not row-wise — three prediction attempts this project (LLM reranker, name<->NAICS embedding mismatch, a PU-bagging learned scorer) were honest NEGATIVES because individual loans look plausible, while the two RETRIEVAL/RELATIONAL wins were LLM entity-resolution (+79 labels) and a name+amount+area similarity homophily test (~3.4x). This loop generalizes the relational signal into a graph. HONEST CALLBACK (do not forget): the single-edge-type version `duplicate_address_ring` (shared address ALONE) was already validated NULL — legitimate co-location (office parks, strip malls, registered agents) dominates. So the BET here is that COMBINING edge types (address + same-entity + name/amount/area similarity) plus community detection separates real rings from benign clustering. An honest NEGATIVE is an acceptable outcome — document it. DISPOSITION: EXPLORATORY only (SIGN-010) — never auto-promote into registry.all_detectors() or the production composite; that is a MANUAL human decision after real-data validation, done AFTER this loop. BRANCH NOTE: this loop runs on the PR branch `m7-tier1-and-m8-ai-followups` because the dependencies it reuses (src/relief_probe/similarity/core.py, benchmark.core.temporal_label_split, src/relief_probe/embeddings.py) live ONLY on this branch, not on main. REUSE (exact): detectors/_address.py::normalize_address(address, city, state, zip) -> building-level key; detectors/_entity.py::entity_key(name, address, city, state, zip) -> normalized name@address; similarity/core.py::find_similar (blocking-first name+$+area look-alikes; or reuse its blocking/embedding internals) and embeddings.py (HashingEmbedder default offline; Model2VecEmbedder behind embeddings-lite); benchmark/core.py::temporal_label_split(con, year) -> (train,test) by charge_date and ranking_metrics/positive_rank_stats; stats.py::robust_z. GUARDRAILS (see plans/guardrails.md SIGN-### + these): (1) NEVER run the relief-probe CLI or open data/relief_probe.duckdb in tests — every test seeds a tmp_path warehouse via warehouse.connect(...). (2) Graph FEATURES MUST BE LABEL-FREE — never read fraud_cases (or any label table) to compute a feature/signal (that would leak + inherit prosecution bias); ONLY the G-003 validation SCRIPT may read labels, exactly like benchmark. Prove label-freeness with a test on an EMPTY fraud_cases table. (3) Scale by SPARSITY: build edges only WITHIN blocks (group loans by a shared key, link within the group) and CAP huge groups (a common address/lender shared by thousands must not form a million-edge clique — skip or cap groups above a size threshold). networkx is pure-python; the $150k+ slice is ~965k nodes, so keep edges sparse. (4) networkx is imported LAZILY behind the `graph` extra; a missing extra raises a clear, actionable RuntimeError (mirror the embeddings/agent extra pattern). (5) No invented benchmark NUMBERS in docs — qualitative only; real numbers come from the G-003 validation run (a MANUAL post-loop step) or are reported honestly if the loop runs it. (6) Entity key is loan_number (str). (7) Style: from __future__ import annotations, typed, docstrings, ruff line-length 90. Commit ONE feature per iteration; set passes=true; update plans/progress.md (Learnings); re-read plans/guardrails.md each iteration.

features.title: Multi-relational loan graph builder (NetworkX, graph extra)
acceptanceCriteria: Add a `graph = ["networkx>=3.0"]` optional-dependency extra to pyproject.toml (lightweight, pure-python, NO torch).

New package src/relief_probe/graph/ (with __init__.py) + src/relief_probe/graph/build.py defining build_loan_graph(con, *, min_amount=150_000.0, edge_types=("address","entity","similarity"), max_group=50, ...) that returns a NetworkX Graph. Nodes = loan_numbers in the $150k+ slice (read-only). Each edge carries a `type` attribute in {address, entity, similarity}.

Edges are LABEL-FREE and SPARSE-by-blocking: (a) address edges link loans sharing a normalized building-level address (reuse detectors/_address.normalize_address); (b) entity edges link loans sharing a normalized entity key (reuse detectors/_entity.entity_key) — i.e. same resolved borrower (duplicate funding); (c) similarity edges link loans that are high name + amount-band + same-area look-alikes (reuse similarity/core blocking + embeddings; a default offline HashingEmbedder is fine). Within each shared-key GROUP, link members; SKIP/cap groups larger than max_group so a giant shared key cannot form a huge clique.

networkx is imported LAZILY inside the function; if the `graph` extra is absent a clear RuntimeError is raised (mirror embeddings/agent gating). Read-only; never writes to the warehouse; works on a warehouse with an EMPTY fraud_cases table (label-free).

tests/test_graph_build.py on a seeded tmp_path warehouse (empty fraud_cases): a planted ring (loans sharing an address + near-identical names + close amounts) forms ONE connected component spanning >=2 edge types; unrelated loans stay isolated/sparse; edge `type` attributes are present; a group above max_group is capped (not a full clique); the builder never queries fraud_cases.
Verification passes (uv run --extra vision --extra graph pytest && uvx ruff check .)
features.notes: Keep it sparse: GROUP BY a normalized key in Python, link within the group, cap group size. Reuse normalize_address / entity_key verbatim. For the similarity edge, reuse the similarity blocking (same-state + amount band) and an embedder cosine threshold; the offline HashingEmbedder keeps tests dependency-free. Store node attributes for borrower_state/amount/name if useful downstream.

features.title: Label-free structural features + exploratory ring detector
acceptanceCriteria: src/relief_probe/graph/features.py::graph_structural_features(graph) -> dict[str, dict] mapping each loan_number to LABEL-FREE structural features: component_size (connected-component size), degree, n_address_edges / n_entity_edges / n_similarity_edges (degree by edge type), distinct_borrowers (count of distinct normalized borrower names in the component), and community_size (size of its community via networkx.community greedy_modularity_communities or label_propagation_communities, computed only on components above a small size to stay cheap).

An EXPLORATORY detector (src/relief_probe/detectors/ or graph/): MultiRelationalRingDetector(Detector) with detector_id='fraud_ring_graph' that emits a Signal for every loan in a component spanning >= 2 distinct edge TYPES and >= min_borrowers distinct borrowers (constructor params); score is a label-free structural quantity (e.g. log distinct_borrowers, or community_size) monotonic in ring strength. Read-only; LABEL-FREE (never reads fraud_cases).

Registered in registry.exploratory_detectors() ONLY — all_detectors() UNCHANGED (SIGN-010); get_detector('fraud_ring_graph') resolves it; registry docstring updated (built + tested; pending real-data validation).

tests/test_graph_features.py (seeded tmp_path, EMPTY fraud_cases): structural features computed correctly on a planted multi-edge-type ring (component_size, distinct_borrowers, edge-type counts); the detector fires on the ring's loans and stays quiet on isolated loans; 'fraud_ring_graph' is in exploratory_detectors() and NOT in all_detectors(); the detector returns results without ever querying fraud_cases.
Verification passes
features.notes: Mirror the H6/Loop1/Loop2/Loop3 disposition. The detector is the per-loan surface of the graph; features.py is the reusable structural-feature provider. Keep community detection cheap (only on components above a small threshold). LABEL-FREE is the cardinal rule — prove it with an empty-fraud_cases test.

features.title: Validation: does ring/community structure concentrate prosecuted loans?
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/validate_ring_graph.py — READ-ONLY against the real warehouse (connect(read_only=True)). Build the multi-relational graph over the $150k+ slice (or a documented sample for tractability), compute the LABEL-FREE structural ring score per loan, rank loans by it, and measure whether prosecuted labels concentrate at the top vs base rate — using the H7 temporal holdout (benchmark.temporal_label_split: evaluate on labels charged > a holdout year the structure never used). Report recall@k / mean-percentile (benchmark.ranking_metrics + positive_rank_stats) and compare against the composite on the SAME held-out labels.

Mirror scripts/validate_naics_mismatch.py structure + its HONEST subsample/segment caveat in the module docstring: a graph over a sample inflates density; read the CONTRAST (does structure beat base rate / composite), not absolute lift; an honest NEGATIVE (ring structure no better than the composite / chance) is a valid, documented outcome.

Script runs end-to-end on the real warehouse and prints a clear verdict (structure concentrates prosecuted loans BETTER / NO BETTER than chance and vs the composite). It MUST NOT write to the warehouse and MUST NOT use labels to compute the structural score (only to evaluate).
The script imports cleanly and `uvx ruff check .` passes; add a tiny pure-helper unit test if helpful (guarded so it does not need the real warehouse).
Verification passes
features.notes: This is the honest gate. Label-free structural score for ranking (e.g. distinct_borrowers in the component, community_size, or similarity-edge degree). Use temporal_label_split so the evaluation is out-of-time. The real-warehouse run is the manual artifact; the unit-testable part is the import/smoke + any pure helper.

features.title: Docs: catalog the fraud-ring graph layer (qualitative)
acceptanceCriteria: README: add the graph layer to the architecture + a catalog entry for the EXPLORATORY fraud_ring_graph detector / graph layer — explain the multi-relational design (address + entity + similarity edges, community detection), WHY a graph (relational/coordinated fraud the row-wise model misses), the HONEST address-alone-null callback (duplicate_address_ring was null; this combines edges), and honest false-positive modes (legitimate co-location: office parks, strip malls, registered agents, shared lenders). NO benchmark/lift numbers.

NEXT_STEPS.md: add a 'Loop 4 / graph' entry — built exploratory; the relational thesis; the MANUAL post-loop validation step (run scripts/validate_ring_graph.py on the real warehouse, measure held-out concentration vs the composite, promote only on independent lift). Qualitative only.
No fabricated metrics; qualitative only.
Verification passes
features.notes: Docs only. Record the relational thesis + the address-alone-null callback so it is not re-litigated. Keep all numbers out (they come from the manual G-003 run).
